title: Smoky Turkish Lamb Adana Kebap
description: A garlicky and tender Turkish dish featuring lamb, golden caster sugar, and smoky binchotan-charcoal flavors, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and can be made without onion and garlic for those with sensitivities
instructions: Preheat the binchotan-charcoal grill to medium-high heat, aiming for 200C
In a large bowl, combine the lamb mince, golden caster sugar, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking
Grill the kebaps for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are cooked through and slightly charred
Brush the kebaps with lemon juice during the last 2 minutes of cooking
Serve the Adana Kebap hot, garnished with chopped parsley and your choice of sides
